Quote.title: Quote
Quote.description: Represents a sales quote in the system.

customerName.description: Name of the customer associated with the quote.

description.description: A detailed description of the quote.

amount.description: The total amount of the quote.

status.description: The current status of the quote (e.g., New, Pending, Approved, Loss, Rejected).

rejectionReason.description: The reason for rejection, if the quote is rejected.

followUpHistory.description: An array of follow-up events for this quote.

createdAt.description: The date and time the quote was created.

definition.description: Stores sales quotes with Kanban status. Supports QAPs through the `status` field. The entire document is self-contained for authorization.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to manage sales quotes and their status within a Kanban board. A single collection `quotes` stores all quote documents. The structure ensures authorization independence by not relying on hierarchical data access. Security rules can be written to allow only authorized users (e.g., sales representatives) to create, read, update, and delete quotes. Segregation is implicitly achieved as all quotes reside in the same collection with similar access needs (subject to status-based rules). The `status` field is used for QAPs. Follow-up history is embedded within the quote document.
